Based on the search results, I've found information about Structured Text programming support for the Mitsubishi Q04UDVCPU:

## Q04UDVCPU Structured Text Support

The Q04UDVCPU supports Structured Text (ST) programming along with Ladder diagram (LD), Logic symbolic language, and Function Block Diagram (FBD).

The Q04UDVCPU is a Universal Programmable Logic Controller (PLC) CPU unit with 40Ksteps / 160KBytes program memory capacity and 4096 x I/O points connection capacity.

## Available Documentation

The search results indicate that a Mitsubishi Q04UDVCPU Structured Text Programming Guide Book is available, along with other related documentation such as the Common Instructions Programming Manual and PID Control Instructions Programming Manual.

## Programming Tools

For developing Structured Text programs for Mitsubishi PLCs like the Q04UDVCPU, Mitsubishi GX Developer, GX Works 2, and GX Works 3 can be used for making programs in Structured text.

The Q04UDVCPU is part of the MELSEC-Q series and fully supports Structured Text (ST) as one of its programming languages, conforming to IEC 61131-3 standards.

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about Mitsubishi QCPU Structured Text and GX Works2 requirements and limitations:

## Key Requirements and Limitations

**Applicable CPU Modules:**
The QCPU structured programming is applicable to specific CPU module models for structured projects, including various Q series processors. Examples include Q00JCPU, Q00CPU and Q01CPU of function version B or later, Q02(H)CPU, Q06CPU, Q12HCPU and Q25HCPU, Q02PHCPU, Q06PHCPU, Q12PHCPU and Q25PHCPU, and Q12PRHCPU and Q25PRHCPU.

**Device Limitations:**
For Universal model QCPU/LCPU, Z16 to Z19 cannot be used in Structured Text programs.

**File Size Constraints:**
The file size per program is 839680 characters.

**Label Name Requirements:**
Label names must be entered within 16 characters.

**Programming Tools:**
GX Works2 is the programming tool used to create, edit and/or monitor ST programs.

**Language Features:**
Structured Text (ST) is a high-level structured text language with grammatical structure similar to C language.

The search results contain references to official Mitsubishi manuals on these topics, which provide more detailed specifications if needed.

data.query: Mitsubishi Q04UDVCPU specifications programming languages GX Works2
Mitsubishi Q04UDVCPU specifications programming languages GX Works2

content.content:
# Mitsubishi PLC Structured Text Support

## Supported PLC Models

The document specifies that **Q series and L series PLCs** support Structured Text programming. Specifically mentioned are:
- "Q CPU-Q mode or LCPU" for both GX Developer and GX Works environments

## GX Works Versions

Two software platforms are identified for ST development:
- **GX Developer**: Requires selecting "structured text features" during installation
- **GX Works 2**: Native ST support without special configuration

The document does not mention version numbers or GX Works 3 capabilities for ST specifically.

## Project Configuration

For both GX Developer and GX Works 2, ST project setup requires:
- Selecting the CPU type (Q CPU-Q mode or LCPU)
- Choosing PLC model
- Setting "use label" in label settings (GX Developer)
- Selecting **ST as the program language**

## Limitations

The document notes: "the MELSEC-Q/L series also supports languages other than the ST, the language adequate for processing can be used." This indicates ST is optional rather than mandatory, but no explicit limitations are stated for Q series Universal CPUs.

No information about Universal CPU restrictions appears in the provided content.

Based on the web search results, here's information about the Mitsubishi Q04UDVCPU specifications, programming languages, GX Works2, and structured projects:

## Q04UDVCPU Specifications

The Q04UDVCPU is a Universal Programmable Logic Controller (PLC) CPU unit with 40Ksteps / 160KBytes program memory capacity, connection capacity of 4096 x I/O points, with USB connector + RJ45 port (10/100Mbit Ethernet), and Ethernet communication capability.

## Programming Languages Supported

The Q04UDVCPU supports Ladder diagram (LD) / Relay symbol language, Structured Text (ST), Logic symbolic language, Function Block Diagram (FBD), MELSAP3 (SFC), and MELSAP-L.

Alternatively, Mitsubishi PLCs can be programmed using IEC languages such as the ladder, sequential function chart (SFC), structured text (ST), and function block diagram (FBD).

## GX Works2 Support

GX Works2 supports the Universal model high-speed type QCPU including the Q04UDVCPU, with support provided by GX Works2 version 1.98C and higher.

## Structured Projects

GX Works2 deploys the global mainstream concepts of "grouping" and "structuring" for fundamental improvement of programming efficiency. The software supports both Simple and Structured project types for creating programs with the Q04UDVCPU.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

- **Q04UDVCPU**
- Q06UDVCPU
- Q13UDVCPU
- Q26UDVCPU

## Version Requirement
"GX Works2 version 1.98C and higher" supports these Universal model processors.

## Project Types and Programming Languages
The document does not specify different project types (Simple vs Structured) or detailed programming language support information. It only notes that GX Works2 is "International Standard IEC 61131-3 compliant" and emphasizes using "grouping" and "structuring" concepts for programming efficiency, but specific project configuration options and language choices are not detailed in this excerpt.

content.text:
충분한 정보가 모였습니다. 결과를 정리하겠습니다.

---

## Mitsubishi Q04UDVCPU ST(Structured Text) 지원 여부 조사 결과

### 1. ST 프로그래밍 지원 여부: 지원함

Q04UDVCPU는 MELSEC-Q 시리즈 Universal 모델 고속 CPU로, 다음 프로그래밍 언어를 모두 지원합니다:

- **Ladder Diagram (LD)** / 릴레이 심볼
- **Structured Text (ST)** -- IEC 61131-3 준거
- **Function Block Diagram (FBD)**
- **SFC (MELSAP3)**
- **MELSAP-L**

### 2. 필요한 소프트웨어: GX Works2

| 항목 | 내용 |
|------|------|
| **소프트웨어** | GX Works2 |
| **최소 버전** | Version 1.98C 이상 |
| **프로젝트 타입** | **Structured Project** 선택 필요 (Simple Project에서는 ST 불가) |
| **CPU 선택** | Q CPU (Q mode) 선택 후 Q04UDVCPU 모델 지정 |

- **GX Developer**에서도 ST가 가능하지만, 설치 시 "Structured Text features" 옵션을 별도로 선택해야 합니다.
- **GX Works3**는 iQ-R / iQ-F 시리즈 전용이므로 Q 시리즈에는 사용 불가합니다.

### 3. 제한사항 및 요구사항

| 제한 항목 | 내용 |
|-----------|------|
| **인덱스 레지스터** | Universal 모델 QCPU에서 ST 프로그램 내 Z16~Z19 사용 불가 |
| **프로그램 파일 크기** | 프로그램당 최대 839,680자 |
| **라벨명 길이** | 최대 16자 |
| **메모리 용량** | Q04UDVCPU는 40K 스텝 / 160KB 프로그램 메모리 (ST는 래더보다 메모리 소비가 크므로 주의) |
| **프로젝트 타입** | 반드시 "Structured Project"로 생성해야 ST 언어 선택 가능 |

### 요약

Q04UDVCPU에서 ST 프로그래밍은 완전히 지원됩니다. **GX Works2 v1.98C 이상**에서 **Structured Project**로 생성하면 됩니다. 다만 인덱스 레지스터 Z16~Z19 사용 제한과 프로그램 메모리 크기(40K 스텝)를 고려하여 대규모 ST 프로그램 작성 시 메모리 관리에 유의해야 합니다.

---

Sources:
- [Mitsubishi Q04UDVCPU 제품 페이지](https://shop1.us.mitsubishielectric.com/products/Q04UDVCPU)
- [GX Works2 지원 CPU 목록 (공식)](https://www.mitsubishielectric.com/fa/products/cnt/plceng/smerit/gx_works2/support/index.html)
- [Structured Text (ST) Programming Guide Book (공식 PDF)](https://dl.mitsubishielectric.com/dl/fa/document/manual/plc/sh080368e/sh080368ei.pdf)
- [GX Works2 Structured Project Operating Manual (공식 PDF)](https://dl.mitsubishielectric.com/dl/fa/document/manual/plc/sh080781eng/sh080781engag.pdf)
- [Mitsubishi PLC Structured Text 개요](https://plcblog.in/plc/Mitsubishi/Mitsubishi%20PLC%20Structured%20Text.php)
- [Basic Structured Text Programming in Mitsubishi PLCs](https://control.com/technical-articles/basic-structured-text-programming-in-mitsubishi-plcs/)
- [QCPU Structured Programming Manual](https://documents.mrosupply.com/product_documents/63/75/6375394/manual_SHNA-080782ENG.pdf)
